tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: From Hospital Bed to World Cup Starter: Giuliano Simeone's Three-Year Journey
stories.summary: Giuliano Simeone fulfilled a hospital bed promise by starting for Argentina at the World Cup, playing 71 minutes against Jordan three years after a devastating injury.

stories.headline: Underdog Goalkeeper Defeats His Idol: Paraguay Stuns Germany in World Cup Shootout
stories.summary: Young Paraguay goalkeeper Orlando Gill stopped his idol Manuel Neuer in a penalty shootout that sent four-time champion Germany home.

stories.headline: Theatre Friends Secretly Raise Funds to Buy Patricia Her First Reliable Car
stories.summary: Patricia Alexander, who quietly organized costumes for years, was surprised by theatre friends who secretly raised funds to buy her a dependable used vehicle.

stories.headline: Puffins Thrive Again: Lundy Island's Seabirds Rise from the Brink
stories.summary: After 20 years of conservation, puffins, razorbills, and guillemots on Lundy Island reached over 40,000—their highest numbers since the 1930s.

stories.headline: School Resource Officer Rents Theatre for 144 Students After Field Trip Falls Through
stories.summary: Officer Sean Reavie rented all 144 seats at a Phoenix theatre so seventh and eighth graders could see Toy Story 5, with several experiencing their first movie theatre visit.

stories.headline: Two Heroes Refuse Evacuation to Feed Abandoned Pets
stories.summary: Diedra Villeneuve and Derek Mckillop stayed behind during evacuation, driving through the village with donated dog food to care for abandoned pets.

stories.headline: Sea Turtles Return in Record Numbers: Tybee Island Celebrates Biggest Nesting Season Ever
stories.summary: Tybee Island celebrates record-breaking 39 sea turtle nests, proving three decades of dedicated protection efforts are finally bearing fruit.

stories.headline: Serena Williams Returns to Singles Play at 44, Inspiring a Generation
stories.summary: Playing her first singles match in four years at 44, Serena Williams reminded the world that joy and passion for the game matter more than wins or losses.

stories.headline: Scientists Grow Eye Cells That Restore Sight in Mice
stories.summary: Duke engineers grew specialized retinal blood vessel cells from stem cells that regenerated damaged tissue and fully restored vision in mice.

stories.headline: Restaurant Opens Doors Free to Wildfire Victims, Prioritizing Healing Over Profit
stories.summary: Restaurant owner Jennifer Priest opened her doors to wildfire victims with free meals and gathering space, choosing community care over business concerns.

stories.headline: New T-Cell Therapy Shows Early Promise Against Deadly Childhood Brain Cancers
stories.summary: Children's National Hospital's phase 1 trial of a new T-cell therapy showed lasting clinical benefit in children with deadly brain tumors like DIPG, offering hope where few options existed.

stories.headline: Philippine Tamaraws Keep Climbing Back: Population Hits 425
stories.summary: Critically endangered Philippine tamaraws celebrate a 12% population increase, with young animals appearing in areas that sat empty just months ago.

stories.headline: Young Gharial Spotted: River Ecosystem in Assam Healing at Last
stories.summary: A juvenile gharial spotted in Assam's Kekaidong River signals successful breeding and the healing of the state's riverine ecosystem after decades of decline.

stories.headline: Italy's Historic Narrow Tracks Get a Clean Future: Hydrogen Trains Arrive
stories.summary: Sardinia will run the world's first hydrogen trains on its historic 950mm narrow-gauge tracks from the 1800s, eliminating over 2,100 tons of CO2 yearly.
